description: Interest calculated on both principal and accumulated interest — the most powerful force in personal finance
example: $10,000 at 7% annual return for 30 years = $76,123 (vs $31,000 simple interest)
rule_of_72: Divide 72 by interest rate to find years to double money (72/7% ≈ 10 years)
key_insight: Starting early dramatically outweighs contributing more later (time > amount)

description: Liquid savings reserve covering 3-6 months of essential living expenses
how_to_build: Automate monthly transfer; start with $1,000 goal, then build to full amount
why_critical: Prevents debt spiral when unexpected expenses occur (job loss, medical, car repair)

description: Passive investing in funds tracking a market index (e.g., S&P 500)
vs_active: Average active fund underperforms index after fees over 10+ year periods

types.HSA: health savings; triple tax advantage; 2024 limit $4,150 individual

types.529: education savings; state tax deduction; tax-free growth for education

priority_order: 1. 401k to employer match → 2. HSA → 3. Roth IRA → 4. Max 401k → 5. Taxable brokerage
key_benefit: Tax-deferred or tax-free growth significantly boosts long-term returns

types.good_debt: mortgage, student loans, business loans — productive use, often lower rates

types.bad_debt: credit cards, payday loans, car loans for depreciating assets — high rates

credit_card_trap: 18-28% APR; minimum payments extend payoff by years
debt_to_income: Keep below 36% for financial health; mortgage lenders prefer <28% for housing costs

description: Dividing investment portfolio among asset classes to balance risk and return
age_rule: Traditional: 110 minus age = % in stocks; modern: 120-age (longer life expectancy)
rebalancing: Annually or when allocation drifts >5% from target

description: Numerical representation of creditworthiness (300-850); used for loans, rentals, employment
free_monitoring: AnnualCreditReport.com (free official); Credit Karma, Experian app

key_terms: deductible: what you pay before insurance kicks in
OOP max: maximum you pay per year
disability_importance: 40% chance of 3+ month disability before age 65; income is your greatest asset
life_insurance: Term life preferred over whole life for most people; 10-12x annual income in coverage

description: General increase in price level reducing purchasing power over time
US_average: ~3% historically; peaked 9.1% in June 2022, moderating to ~3% by 2024
rule_of_72: At 3% inflation, purchasing power halves in ~24 years

description: Study of psychological influences on financial decisions
biases: loss aversion: losses feel 2x worse than equivalent gains
mental accounting: treating money differently based on source
consequences: holding losing stocks too long (disposition effect)
